dialogue.intro: My charm slipped loose mid-dance, right where the old stump keeps its rings — without it my flame gutters lower every hour. Find it before the paths close, won't you?
dialogue.reminder: Any luck by the stump? I can feel my light dimming...
dialogue.thanks: You found it — oh, thank the embers! I can feel my flame steady again. When the crossroads closes tonight, I'll dance one more time before I go, for you.
dialogue.farewell: The path home is opening. Dance well, little wanderer — and thank you, truly.

dialogue.intro: I chart my way home by moonlight caught in glass — and mine slipped from my paw into the still water east of here. Without it, I can't find my way through the dark between worlds.
dialogue.reminder: The water still holds it, I think. Or... I hope.
dialogue.thanks: There it is — my little piece of the moon. I can find my way again. You've done more for a stranger than most of my own court would.
dialogue.farewell: The moon is rising on my side of the crossroads. Travel safely, and remember — not all shadows are unkind.

dialogue.intro: I've been cataloguing the Hollow for three years and I still lose my place constantly — my good bookmark slipped from my journal somewhere past the ninth stone on the path. Silly, I know, to need a pressed leaf so badly.
dialogue.reminder: Still no leaf? My journal keeps flopping open to the wrong page without it.
dialogue.thanks: My bookmark — wonderful! Now my notes on tonight's crossing will actually stay in order. You should know, this is the first time in three years anyone's helped me with anything.
dialogue.farewell: I'll write about you in my journal, whoever you are. Safe travels, and thank you for humoring an over-organized scholar.

dialogue.intro: Mochi... not feel whole. Bead rolled away, somewhere flowers grow. Mochi feel wobbly without it. Please find?
dialogue.thanks: Bead! Mochi whole again! Mochi say... thank you, friend. Mochi remember this.
dialogue.farewell: Mochi go home now. Mochi happy. Mochi... will miss new friend.
